name: Moses
shortDescription: Moses is the most important prophet in Judaism, leading the Israelites out of slavery in Egypt and receiving the Ten Commandments from God on Mount Sinai. He is revered as a lawgiver and founder of the Jewish nation.
description: Moses is a central figure in Judaism, Christianity, and Islam. Born during a time when the Pharaoh of Egypt ordered the killing of all newborn Israelite boys, Moses was saved by his mother, who placed him in a basket on the Nile River. He was found by Pharaoh's daughter and raised as an Egyptian prince. As an adult, Moses witnessed the suffering of his people and killed an Egyptian overseer who was beating an Israelite slave. He fled to Midian, where he married Zipporah and became a shepherd. God appeared to Moses in a burning bush and commanded him to return to Egypt and free the Israelites from slavery. After a series of plagues inflicted on Egypt, Moses led the Exodus, parting the Red Sea to allow the Israelites to escape Pharaoh's pursuing army. He then led them through the desert for 40 years, receiving the Ten Commandments and the Torah from God on Mount Sinai. Moses did not enter the Promised Land himself, dying on Mount Nebo after seeing it from afar. He is remembered as the greatest prophet in Judaism, a lawgiver, and a leader who shaped the identity of the Jewish people.
abilities: Miracle-working (staff turning into snake, parting the Red Sea)

sources.title: Ancient Israel: From Abraham to the Roman Destruction of the Temple
sources.author: Hershel Shanks
